title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 210.183 - Alleged perpetrator to be provided written description of investigation process
text:
210.183.  Alleged perpetrator to be provided written description of investigation process. — 1.  At the time of the initial investigation of a report of child abuse or neglect, the division employee conducting the investigation shall provide the alleged perpetrator with a written description of the investigation process.  Such written notice shall be given substantially in the following form:
  2.  If the division uses the family assessment approach, the division shall at the time of the initial contact provide the parent of the child with the following information:
  (1)  The purpose of the contact with the family;
  (2)  The name of the person responding and his or her office telephone number;
  (3)  The assessment process to be followed during the division's intervention with the family including the possible services available and expectations of the family.
(L. 1986 S.B. 470 § 2, A.L. 1994 S.B. 595, A.L. 2004 H.B. 1453, A.L. 2007 S.B. 25, A.L. 2014 H.B. 1092 merged with S.B. 869) 
*Word "and" appears here in original rolls.
